question: In the deprecated `camunda.client.zeebe` namespace, which property is the documented replacement for `camunda.client.zeebe.base-url`?

a.text: The `camunda.client.zeebe` deprecation table maps `base-url` to `camunda.client.rest-address` — the unified REST endpoint for the Camunda 8 client.

b.text: `camunda.client.grpc-address` is the replacement for the separate `camunda.client.zeebe.grpc-address` property, not for `base-url`.

c.text: `cloud.base-url` is itself a deprecated SaaS-composition property, replaced by `cloud.domain`; it is not the replacement for `zeebe.base-url`.

d.text: There is no `camunda.client.zeebe.address` property in the reference; the Zeebe-specific namespace is being phased out, not renamed in place.

explanation: Camunda 8 unifies REST traffic for the orchestration cluster behind a single endpoint, configured by `camunda.client.rest-address`. The Zeebe-specific `base-url` from the deprecated namespace maps onto that unified address.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot Starter - properties reference
